generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts electrical input current (I_M) into rotational torque (τ_M) for the robot's vertical axis"
]

instance Gearbox : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Comprises a standard gearbox (τ_G) followed by a cycloidal gearbox with final output torque (τ_out)"
]

instance SafetyCell : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Restricts working range to [-90°, 90°] and maximum speed to 75°/s for operational safety"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures electrical input current (I_M) to the permanent magnet synchronous motor"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Directly measures motor angle via encoder mounted on the PM synchronous motor"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngleData
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures gearwheel angle via encoder mounted behind the gearbox to reduce backlash"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxAngleData
]

instance GearboxTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures torque (τ_G) transmitted through the gearbox components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxTorqueData
]

instance CycloidalGearboxSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures angular velocity (ω) of the cycloidal gearbox output"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CycloidalSpeedData
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxTorqueSensor
]

instance CycloidalSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CycloidalGearboxSpeedSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBDDModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram describing the robot's first vertical axis (motor, gearbox, cycloidal gear) and their interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SimulinkCoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model simulating Coulomb-Viscous friction behavior for the gear transmission"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkStribeckModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction model for dynamic analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkLuGreModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink LuGre friction model for comprehensive dynamic behavior simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from motor current sensor (I_M) linked to its source encoder"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from motor position encoder linked to its source"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTEnabler
]

instance MeasuredGearboxAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from gearbox position encoder linked to its source"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTEnabler
]

instance MeasuredGearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from gearbox torque sensor linked to its source"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output from Coulomb-Viscous friction simulation model (τ̇_M) for comparison with measured data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkCoulombViscousFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearboxVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output from simulation models (ω̇_G) representing ideal gearbox behavior"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkLuGreModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides structural modeling capabilities via SysML Block Definition Diagrams for robot axis representation"
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables behavioral simulations using MathWorks Simulink for friction and dynamics modeling"
]

instance MQTTEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data streaming protocol (MQTT) for sensor data integration with digital twin"
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time identification and continuous improvement of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models using live sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides WhiteBoxFrictionInsight, BlackBoxFrictionInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard visualization of measured vs. simulated torque/velocity data traces"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationDashboardInsight
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustments to robot control algorithms based on friction analysis insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAlgorithmAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance WhiteBoxFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction losses via model discrepancies between measured and simulated data"
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven identification of friction characteristics using machine learning models trained on live sensor data"
]

instance VisualizationDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison of measured vs. simulated torque/velocity data for operational analysis"
]

instance ControlAlgorithmAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of robot control algorithms to improve motion accuracy based on friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ProactiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of deviations to enable proactive maintenance (tool changes, wear management)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Iterative refinement of white-box and black-box friction models using live operational data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTraceabilityAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ensuring metadata provenance and model consistency through traceability links"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time (low-latency)** with:
  - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, angular velocity) is sampled and streamed at **high rates** (implied by live operational context).
  - **Model update frequency**: Friction models are dynamically refined during operation, with updates triggered by deviations between measured and simulated data.
  - **Synchronization**: Data traces are synchronized with the physical system’s operational timeline to ensure temporal coherence."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation comprises:
  - **Centralized Digital Shadow**: A unified representation of the robot’s first axis, integrating structural (SysML), behavioral (Simulink), and sensor data.
  - **Processing Pipeline**: A **Processing component** (implied by traceability in the Digital Shadow Reference Model) that consumes input data traces (e.g., _I_M, _ω_M) and produces output traces (e.g., simulated friction-adjusted torque/velocity).
  - **Asset Model (Mabi)**: The physical robot axis (motor, gearbox) as the primary asset, with linked digital models and data traces."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates across the following life-cycle stages:
  - **As-designed (ideal)**: Structural and behavioral models (e.g., SysML SysML Block Definition Diagrams) define the robot’s theoretical behavior without friction.
  - **As-manufactured**: Real-time sensor data and live measurements are integrated to capture operational conditions (e.g., wear, temperature effects).
  - **As-operated**: Continuous model refinement and friction analysis occur during robot operation to adapt to dynamic conditions (e.g., tool changes, wear)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**: Simulation models (e.g., ideal gearbox vs. friction models) are validated against measured data to detect discrepancies.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: Friction models account for dynamic factors (e.g., load, temperature) via configurable parameters (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous model coefficients).
  - **Verification Mechanisms**: Traceability links (e.g., origins of data points) enable auditing of model inputs/outputs.
  - **Error Mitigation**: Backlash reduction via encoder comparisons minimizes positional uncertainty."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection relies on:
  - **MQTT Protocol**: Real-time data streaming from sensors (e.g., motor current, angular velocity) to the digital shadow.
  - **SysML Block Definition Diagrams**: Structural modeling of the robot’s first axis (motor, gearbox) for data linkage.
  - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink**: Simulation models for friction behavior and system dynamics."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT leverages:
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: For structural and behavioral modeling.
  - **MQTT Protocol**: For real-time data communication.
  - **MathWorks Simulink**: For simulation-based modeling (implied by industry-standard tools)."
]
